commit.message:
Add github-read-access, aws-cli, and terraform skills (#6)

Agents lacked reusable skills for GitHub read/clone access, AWS CLI
(CloudFormation), and Terraform — blocking the CEO from inspecting repo
configs and the Tech Lead from infrastructure workflows.

## New Skills

- **`skills/github-read-access`** — read-only `gh` CLI operations: `gh
repo clone`, repo/issue/PR/release/run inspection. Enforces no-write
constraint explicitly.
- **`skills/aws-cli`** — CloudFormation-focused: stack
list/describe/events/drift/template, plus EC2/S3/Lambda/RDS inspection
and Cost Explorer queries. Leads with `aws <service> help` discovery
pattern.
- **`skills/terraform`** — full IaC workflow: init → workspace →
validate/fmt → plan → apply → destroy, state management, outputs,
debugging. Enforces plan-before-apply and no auto-approve on prod.

## Skill Assignments

| Agent | Skills added | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| CEO | `github-read-access` + `exec` unblocked | Read/clone access to
all repos for config inspection |
| Tech Lead | `aws-cli`, `terraform` | Architecture and IaC ownership |
| Cost Controller | `aws-cli` | Cost Explorer queries for `cloud_infra`
spend tracking |

Add comprehensive design docs for all 12 remaining topics

10,543 lines of new system design content across 12 documents,
each with Mermaid diagrams, capacity estimation, real-world
architecture notes, and tiered references.

Fundamentals:
- URL Shortener (807 lines, 6 diagrams) — base62/hash/KGS,
  redirect flow, analytics pipeline, Bitly/t.co references
- Rate Limiter (860 lines, 9 diagrams) — 5 algorithms compared
  (token bucket, leaky bucket, fixed/sliding window), distributed
  rate limiting with Redis Lua scripts
- Key-Value Store (887 lines, 12 diagrams) — consistent hashing,
  LSM tree storage, vector clocks, gossip protocol, Dynamo/Cassandra
- Distributed Cache (961 lines, 14 diagrams) — cache-aside/read-
  through/write-behind patterns, eviction policies, stampede
  prevention, Redis vs Memcached comparison

Messaging & Delivery:
- Message Queue (917 lines, 9 diagrams) — log-structured storage,
  partitioning, ISR replication, exactly-once semantics, Kafka vs
  RabbitMQ vs Pulsar comparison
- Notification System (873 lines, 7 diagrams) — multi-channel
  (push/SMS/email/in-app), priority routing, dedup, APNs/FCM/
  Twilio/SES integration, quiet hours

Social & Search:
- News Feed (819 lines, 6 diagrams) — fan-out on write vs read,
  hybrid approach for celebrities, ML ranking, Twitter/Facebook

- Search Autocomplete (761 lines, 8 diagrams) — trie data

commit.message:
feat(vault): add HA storage with 2 Longhorn replicas (#114)

* feat(vault): add HA storage with 2 Longhorn replicas

## Changes

- Create new `longhorn-hdd-ha` StorageClass with 2 replicas for critical workloads
- Update Vault to use `longhorn-hdd-ha` for data and audit volumes
- Add `replicaAutoBalance: best-effort` to spread replicas across nodes
- Keep existing `longhorn-hdd` with 1 replica for non-critical workloads

## Benefits

- Data redundancy: Vault volumes replicated across 2 nodes
- Protection against single node storage failure
- Reasonable storage overhead: ~120Gi total (fits on 50GB small nodes + 20TB main node)
- Automatic replica spreading via Longhorn's auto-balance

## Storage Capacity

With 2 replicas and 3 Vault pods:
- Data volumes: 3 × 10Gi × 2 = 60Gi
- Audit volumes: 3 × 10Gi × 2 = 60Gi
- Total: 120Gi across HDD-tagged disks

Fits comfortably on:
- shion-ubuntu-2505: 20TB (primary)
- instance-2024-1: 50GB (replica)
- instance-k8s-proxy: 50GB (replica)

## Migration

See vault/README.md for instructions on migrating existing PVCs to gain
2-replica redundancy. Existing volumes will continue to work but won't
automatically gain redundancy until manually updated.

* Apply suggestions from code review

commit.message:
Add Blur annotation tool for pixelating screenshot regions (#33)

* Add Blur annotation tool for pixelating screenshot regions

Introduces a new Blur tool to the annotation toolbar, allowing users to select and blur/pixelate regions of their screenshot. Captures a clean background image of the selection area and applies a blur effect to the chosen region. Updates the UI, shape parameters, and rendering logic to support this feature.

* Add Blur tool tests to AnnotationViewModelTests

Added unit tests for Blur annotation tool to verify correct
shape parameter handling, including edge case for small areas.
Included Blur in drag tool coverage for TryGetShapeParameters.

* Remove unused _backgroundCapture field from OverlayWindow

Refactored OverlayWindow to capture the background image into a local variable instead of storing it in the _backgroundCapture field. This reduces unnecessary class state and simplifies the code by passing the captured image directly to the renderer.

* Replace BlurEffect with true pixelation in the Blur/Pixelate annotation tool (#34)

* Initial plan

* Replace BlurEffect with true pixelation using TransformedBitmap + NearestNeighbor

Resolves the user-facing mismatch where the tooltip said "Blur / Pixelate"
but only a Gaussian BlurEffect was applied. The region is now scaled down
to 1/PixelateBlockSize resolution via TransformedBitmap and rendered at
full size with BitmapScalingMode.NearestNeighbor, producing a mosaic/pixelate
effect. Removes the unused System.Windows.Media.Effects import.

commit.message:
Implement cDAC API GetCCWInterfaces (#124940)

Implements `GetCCWInterfaces` cDAC SOS API

## Description

### Managed contracts
```csharp
// Resolves a COM interface pointer to the ComCallWrapper.
// Returns TargetPointer.Null if interfacePointer is not a recognised COM interface pointer.
public TargetPointer GetCCWFromInterfacePointer(TargetPointer interfacePointer);
// Enumerate the COM interfaces exposed by the ComCallWrapper chain.
// ccw may be any ComCallWrapper in the chain; the implementation navigates to the start.
public IEnumerable<COMInterfacePointerData> GetCCWInterfaces(TargetPointer ccw);
```

### Tests
- Unit tests covering `GetRefCount`, `IsHandleWeak`, standard CCW IP
resolution, SCCW IP resolution, linked wrapper navigation, and
`GetCCWInterfaces` enumeration
- Dump-based integration tests (`BuiltInCOMDumpTests`) with a
Windows-only `CCWInterfaces` debuggee that creates 3 CCWs via `GCHandle`
+ `Marshal.GetIUnknownForObject`; tests use cDAC GC/object contracts to
locate CCWs via strong handles
- `BuiltInCOM_CCW_HasInterfaces` — asserts ≥ 3 CCW pointers found and
each has ≥ 1 interface entry
- `BuiltInCOM_CCW_HasInterfaces_MethodTableReadable` — asserts non-null
`MethodTable` entries can be resolved via
`IRuntimeTypeSystem.GetTypeHandle` with `GetBaseSize > 0`

## Testing

Existing unit tests and new targeted tests all pass. Dump tests are
Windows-only and gated with `[SkipOnOS(IncludeOnly = "windows")]`.

* fix(turbo): reduce concurrency from 18 to 3 to prevent test timeouts

Reduces global task concurrency to 3 to prevent resource exhaustion
when running multiple test suites in parallel. The original concurrency
of 18 caused exit code 130 (SIGINT) timeouts due to:

- Redis connection pool exhaustion
- Database connection limits
- Memory/CPU contention

With concurrency=3, test suites run reliably without timeout failures
while maintaining good parallelism for builds. Exit code 130 failures
are eliminated; only legitimate test failures remain.

Impact: Tests now pass without resource-related timeouts. Slight increase
in overall turbo execution time due to reduced parallelism, but much more
reliable results.

is not available in better-auth@1.4.19. The database schema

when better-auth adds native apiKey plugin support.

* fix(hono-rpc): enable composite mode and declaration generation for project references

Sets "composite": true and enables declaration/declarationMap generation
in packages/hono-rpc/tsconfig.json. This is required when the project is
referenced by other tsconfig files (e.g., tools/cli/tsconfig.json).

Fixes TypeScript error TS6306: Referenced project must have composite: true.

This allows `bun run check:tsconfig` to pass without errors.

- All 6 dev services to run concurrently ✓

* Implement single-path authentication architecture

This commit introduces a unified authentication system that eliminates the dual-path
architecture that was causing 'Unable to mint API...' errors in mobile.

ARCHITECTURE CHANGES:

1. Single credential flow (no more /api/auth/session token minting):
   - Sign-in endpoints (email-otp/verify) are the ONLY place tokens are minted
   - All clients (mobile, web, CLI) receive accessToken + refreshToken at sign-in
   - /api/auth/session is now identity-only (requires Bearer token, returns user info)
   - /api/auth/refresh exchanges refresh tokens for new access tokens

2. Mobile auth provider completely rewritten:
   - Bootstraps from stored API JWT on app reopen (not from Better Auth session)
   - No longer calls /api/auth/session for token minting
   - Uses clean Bearer token pattern for all API calls
   - Handles token refresh transparently when tokens expire

3. API endpoint changes:
   - POST /api/auth/email-otp/verify returns: { user, accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn }
   - POST /api/auth/refresh accepts refreshToken, returns new tokens
   - GET /api/auth/session now requires Authorization Bearer header, returns identity only

4. Protected endpoint middleware unchanged but now correctly enforces:
   - All protected endpoints REQUIRE Bearer token
   - No implicit token derivation or special casing
   - Consistent 401 for invalid/missing tokens

WHY THIS FIXES THE PROBLEM:

- Before: /api/auth/session tried to mint tokens from Better Auth session state
- Better Auth sessions could be stale/unavailable, causing intermittent 500s
- Mobile had no deterministic way to know if it should store tokens or call session endpoint

- Now: Tokens are minted at explicit sign-in time only
- Mobile stores both accessToken and refreshToken after successful sign-in
- All subsequent API calls use Bearer token pattern consistently
- No special-case middleware logic or implicit derivation

API contract is now documented in docs/auth-architecture-single-path.md

All tests pass. Single responsibility principle:
- Sign-in = issue credentials
- Session = check identity
- Refresh = exchange tokens
- Protected endpoints = validate Bearer token

commit.message:
fix: AgentPicker reactivity and deduplication for plugin agents (#121)

## Fixes

- **PowerPoint agent duplicate**: Used reference equality
(\includes(a)\) to detect bundled agents — plugin agents with the same
name are different object instances, so they leaked into Custom. Now
uses **name-based deduplication**.
- **Plugin agents not showing (SPT IQ etc.)**: \plugin.agents\
notification only updated the module-level variable — \AgentPicker\ had
no Zustand subscription to it and never re-rendered. Now calls
\store.setPluginAgents()\ which updates a new **\pluginAgents\ store
field** (ephemeral, not persisted), triggering a reactive re-render.
- **User-imported agents wiped on session start**:
\setImportedAgents(agentConfigs)\ replaced all imported agents with
plugin agents. Fixed: \setPluginAgents\ combines plugin + user-imported
agents when syncing \gentService\.

72/72 affected tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Stefan Broenner <stefan.broenner@microsoft.comm>

commit.message:
fix(test): remove duplicate on_file_select call causing flaky CI

The unstage_hunk test called explorer.on_file_select() for the staged
file immediately after open_codediff_and_wait(), but the explorer
already auto-selects that same file on startup when all changes are
staged. The two concurrent async git operations raced — on slower
Windows/macOS CI runners the callbacks would interfere, leaving
modified_revision unset and failing the assertion.

Remove the redundant on_file_select() call and let the explorer's
built-in auto-selection handle it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: React performance optimization — memoization & render reduction

AppContext (CRITICAL fix):
- Wrapped Provider value with useMemo to prevent creating new object every render
- This eliminates cascading re-renders of ALL context consumers on every 1Hz update
- Capped accidentEvents array at 500 entries to prevent unbounded memory growth

Dashboard, Analytics, LiveMap:
- Wrapped all three pages with React.memo() to skip re-renders when props unchanged
- Analytics: chartData now computed with useMemo (was recalculating 60 items/second)
- LiveMap: trail positions memoized with useMemo

ToastContext (memory leak fix):
- Toast auto-dismiss timeouts now tracked in a Map ref
- All timers properly cleared on component unmount (prevents setState-on-unmounted warnings)

CSS Performance:
- Reduced backdrop-filter blur from 20px to 12px (less GPU compositing work)
- Added will-change: transform to glass-card for GPU layer promotion
